description: Agent lifecycle, worktree management, beads integration, config parsing, and key dispatch are all untested. Add unit and integration tests for these core paths.
title: Expand test coverage for core business logic

close_reason: Fixed: spawn_agent_pty() was missing the ConPTY DSR response (ESC[1;1R) that the integration tests already sent. Added writer.write_all + flush immediately after obtaining the PTY writer, matching the test pattern. This prevents output buffering on Windows when ConPTY waits for the handshake.
description: Integration tests respond to the ESC[6n] DSR query but spawn_agent_pty() in runtime.rs doesn't. Could cause output buffering on Windows when ConPTY waits for the handshake response.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: spawn_agent_pty() in runtime.rs never sent the ESC[1;1R cursor-position response to ConPTY's ESC[6n DSR query on startup, causing output buffering on Windows. The integration tests already did this but the main code path did not. FIX: Added writer.write_all(b"\x1b[1;1R") + flush immediately after obtaining the writer in spawn_agent_pty(). REGRESSION TEST: No new test added — the existing integration tests already cover DSR response behavior. Filed obelisk-yfq for a pre-existing test failure in pty_write_input_and_read_response on macOS.
title: Missing ConPTY DSR response in main code path

description: total_lines in app.rs:2090 counts raw 0x0A bytes in the PTY data stream: agent.total_lines += data.iter().filter(|&&b| b == b'\n').count(). Terminal apps like Claude Code frequently redraw the screen (spinners, progress bars, status updates), re-emitting newlines for the same visual lines on every refresh. This causes the line counter to inflate rapidly — often 10-100x the actual content lines. Additionally, lines_this_tick on line 2096 uses newlines.max(1), adding minimum 1 per 4KB PTY chunk even with zero newlines. The counter is displayed on the Dashboard agent list (ui.rs:1102) and the Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1663). Fix: derive line count from vt100 parser scrollback_len() + screen rows, which tracks actual unique content lines rather than raw byte throughput.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: total_lines accumulated raw 0x0A bytes from PTY stream; terminal redraws re-emit newlines for same visual lines, inflating count 10-100x. lines_this_tick used .max(1) adding phantom lines per chunk. FIX: agent_line_count() now derives from vt100 parser scrollback() + screen rows. Removed raw newline accumulation. Removed .max(1) inflation. REGRESSION TEST: no — project has no test infrastructure for PTY-based output.
title: Line count increments too fast — counts raw PTY newlines instead of visible lines

description: When an agent finishes its work but the Claude Code process remains alive (idle REPL), the only option is to kill it, which sets exit code non-zero and marks it as Failed. Need a keybinding (e.g. 'D' for done) that: 1) Sets the agent status to Completed (overriding exit code detection), 2) Sends SIGTERM to clean up the process, 3) Triggers normal completion flow (worktree cleanup, session recording, bd close). This avoids the kill-shows-as-failed problem when an agent has genuinely finished but the underlying CLI session hasn't exited.
title: Add manual 'mark complete' action for agents

description: Auto-spawn currently grabs tasks in ready queue order. Should respect issue priority so P0 tasks are spawned before P1, P1 before P2, etc.
title: Priority-aware auto-spawn

description: Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage.
title: Remove unreliable token/cost tracking (PTY scraping too fragile)

close_reason: Completed: replaced tokio full feature flag with specific features (rt-multi-thread, macros, time, sync, process) in Cargo.toml to reduce compile time
description: Currently using tokio with 'full' features. Only rt, time, sync, and process are needed. Reducing feature flags would improve compile time.
title: Slim down tokio feature flags

close_reason: Completed: Replaced 3 unwrap() calls with safe if-let alternatives — 2 on selected_agent_id and 1 on confirm_kill_agent_id in main.rs. No behavior change; UI state desync now silently skips instead of panicking.
description: 2 unwraps on selected_agent_id and confirm_kill_agent_id in main.rs could panic if UI state desyncs. Replace with if-let or match for safe handling.
notes: COMPLETED: Replaced 3 unwrap() calls with if-let in main.rs — 2 on selected_agent_id (lines 240, 732) and 1 on confirm_kill_agent_id (line 400). DECISIONS: Used if-let Some pattern to silently skip when state desyncs rather than panicking.
title: Replace unwrap() calls in hot paths with safe alternatives

description: on_agent_pty_ready() in app.rs:2048 unconditionally writes \x1b[1;1R (cursor position report) to every agent's PTY stdin. This was intended for Windows ConPTY's startup ESC[6n handshake, but on macOS no such query is sent. Claude Code receives the unexpected CPR on stdin and renders it as literal text '^[[1;1R' in the terminal output. Fix: gate the DSR response behind #[cfg(windows)] or, better, intercept ESC[6n from PTY output and respond dynamically.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: on_agent_pty_ready() unconditionally wrote CPR (\x1b[1;1R) to every PTY stdin. On macOS no ESC[6n query exists, so it leaked as visible text. FIX: Removed unconditional CPR; added dynamic ESC[6n interception in on_agent_pty_data(). REGRESSION TEST: Existing pty_write_input_and_read_response test fixed and passes.
title: CPR response ESC[1;1R leaks as visible text on macOS

close_reason: Completed: Added tracing, tracing-subscriber, and tracing-appender for structured file-based logging. Logs to .beads/logs/obelisk.log with daily rotation, non-blocking writes, and configurable level via RUST_LOG. Instrumented startup/shutdown, agent lifecycle, polls, and errors.
description: Add the tracing crate for structured logging. Would help debugging in production without the TUI active, and could log to file alongside the terminal UI.
notes: COMPLETED: Added tracing + tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender. File-based logging to .beads/logs/obelisk.log with daily rotation and non-blocking writes. Instrumented startup/shutdown, agent spawn/exit, poll results, errors. DECISIONS: Used file-only logging since TUI owns stdout/stderr. Default level is info, configurable via RUST_LOG.
title: Add structured logging with tracing crate

close_reason: Fixed: resize_agent_pty() was replacing the vt100 parser with a new instance, losing all scrollback. Now uses screen_mut().set_size() to resize in place, preserving buffer content.
description: vt100 parser is recreated on terminal resize, losing buffer history momentarily. Should preserve scrollback content across resize events.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: resize_agent_pty() replaced the vt100::Parser with a new instance on every resize, destroying all scrollback. FIX: Use parser.screen_mut().set_size(rows, cols) to resize in place, preserving buffer content. REGRESSION TEST: yes - vt100_parser_resize_preserves_scrollback.
title: Scrollback lost on terminal resize

close_reason: Completed: LazyLock<Regex> optimization was already in place (RE_INPUT_TOKENS, RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS in app.rs). Added 7 unit tests validating regex compilation, pattern matching, case insensitivity, and detect_phase ordering.
description: Regex patterns for token parsing are compiled on every 4KB PTY chunk. Compile once at startup using LazyLock or once_cell to avoid repeated overhead.
notes: COMPLETED: Verified LazyLock<Regex> optimization already in place (RE_INPUT_TOKENS, RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS at app.rs:15-20). Added 7 unit tests for token regex parsing and detect_phase. All unit tests pass. DECISIONS: No code change to regex compilation needed — LazyLock was introduced in obelisk-942 and is the correct approach.
title: Optimize regex compilation for token parsing
pty_write_input_and_read_response test fails on macOS — DSR echo contaminates bash read
